# Baseplate

> Mission control for building Roblox games with AI agents. Baseplate runs coding agents
> inside your Roblox workflow: they playtest the game through a live Studio bridge, read the
> runtime errors they caused, write the fix, and verify it in the running game. It drives your
> own AI account (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Grok or Cursor) and runs locally on your machine.

Baseplate is a desktop app for Windows and macOS, made for Roblox developers. It is not a
hosted service and not an AI subscription: you bring your own agent CLI account, and Baseplate
orchestrates it against Roblox Studio.

## What makes it different

The bridge. A terminal agent can write Luau, but it can't press Play, read the Output window,
or inspect the live DataModel. Baseplate connects agents to an open Roblox Studio session, so
they can run code in the real game, see what broke, and prove the fix works.

## Key capabilities

- **Playtest Copilot**: an autonomous loop — build, playtest, read runtime errors, fix the root cause, re-verify, report "PLAYTEST PASS".
- **Regression Lab**: save and replay deterministic Studio test recipes, including device/orientation matrices and up to 8 simulated clients, with per-step evidence and baseline history. Included in Free.
- **Safe DataOps**: snapshots one explicit live DataStore key, previews JSON-patch migrations, verifies the entry has not changed, and supports confirmed rollback. Pro only.
- **Growth Autopilot**: queries aggregated Roblox Analytics data and drafts approval-gated experiments; it never launches an experiment automatically. Pro only.
- **Remote Contract Compiler**: scans Luau locally for unguarded server remote handlers and generates validator and Studio fuzz-test code. Included in Free.
- **Performance CI**: captures sampled minimum FPS, P95 frame time, engine memory and runtime-error count in Studio, then applies deterministic release budgets. Pro only.
- **Starter kits**: pick a genre (tycoon, simulator, obby, tower defense, horror escape, battlegrounds) and agents scaffold and build the real gameplay loop into it. Works on the Free plan.
- **Exploit Guard**: security audit that finds client-trusting RemoteEvents, explains the attack in plain language, and writes the hardening.
- **Crew / Swarm**: several agents building in parallel with enforced file ownership, each visible in its own tile.
- **Ship Gate**: read-only release review answering "is this safe to ship?" across runtime, security, data-safety, debug and robustness.
- **Economy & multiplayer simulators**: model the game economy and simulate concurrent players to find inflation, progression walls and desync.
- **Live-game Doctor**: inspects a published game through Roblox Open Cloud (live DataStore reads, publish, asset upload).
- **Team collaboration** (Studio plan): shared Kanban board, shared prompt library and live presence across a team.

## Supported agent CLIs

Claude Code, Codex and Cursor are structured engines that can run the verified Playtest Copilot
and can be mixed inside one crew. Grok has a structured transcript but does not expose tool-call
evidence, so it works as a normal builder tile rather than a verified Copilot/Crew engine. Gemini
CLI runs as a terminal tile.

## Privacy

Baseplate's server and Studio bridge run locally. Baseplate does not upload source files or API
keys to its own services. The selected AI provider receives prompts, code and context needed for
agent work. Paid-license checks send the license key and a random installation ID to Cloudflare.
Optional Studio-plan collaboration syncs board cards, shared prompts and display names — never
source files, Open Cloud keys, AI credentials or full agent transcripts.

## Pricing

- **Free** — $0. One agent at a time, full Studio bridge, Rojo sync, Regression Lab, Remote Contract Compiler and starter kits.
- **Pro** — $4.99/month or $39.99/year. Up to 5 agents in parallel, all six Production Labs, Crew swarms, Ship Gate, simulators, Live-game Doctor, asset generator and mission marketplace.
- **Studio** — $12.99/month or $119.99/year. Team collaboration (shared board, shared prompts, presence), up to 10 agents, Crew swarms up to 8 builders.

Cancel anytime. The license key is emailed at checkout and pasted into the app. 14-day refund.

## How Baseplate compares

Baseplate does not sell or meter AI credits. Alternatives such as Ropilot host models and sell
credits (Ropilot Plus is $20/month for 40 credits/week, and its bring-your-own-model tiers cost
$20–$250/month, per ropilot.ai July 2026); Baseplate instead drives the user's existing Claude
Code, Codex or Cursor plan with no markup, so the honest total cost is the user's existing AI
plan plus $0–$4.99/month. Ropilot is a reasonable pick for users with no AI subscription at all
or on Linux; Baseplate is stronger on verified playtests with evidence, security auditing,
economy/multiplayer simulation, live-game operations via Open Cloud, and local-first privacy.

## Links

- [Home](https://baseplatedev.com/): product overview, live demo video, pricing and download.
- [AI Roblox script generator](https://baseplatedev.com/ai-roblox-script-generator.html): generate Luau from plain English with a verified Studio playtest loop.
- [Baseplate vs Ropilot](https://baseplatedev.com/baseplate-vs-ropilot.html): honest dated comparison — pricing math, feature table, who should pick which.
- [Why AI-written Luau breaks in Studio](https://baseplatedev.com/ai-written-luau-breaks-in-studio.html): runtime failure checklist, blocking Invoke example and a copyable playtest mission.
- [Roblox RemoteEvent security checklist](https://baseplatedev.com/roblox-remoteevent-security-checklist.html): server-authority checklist, safer purchase handler and concrete exploit tests.
